titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Cathy Courtney collection of artists' books ephemera
Courtney (Cathy) collection of artists' books ephemera
titlestmt.author: Compiled by YCBA Staff, Meg Rinn

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

address.addressline: Department of Rare Books and Manuscripts

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 4, 2023 at 22:01

did.unittitle: Cathy Courtney collection of artists' books ephemera

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

did.abstract: The collection comprises printed and manuscript material concerning contemporary British artists' books, compiled by Cathy Courtney. It includes exhibition invitations and announcements; press releases; prospectuses; catalogs; curricula vitae; correspondence; newsletters; interviews; artist's statements; brochures; and news articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises printed and manuscript material concerning contemporary British artists' books, compiled by Cathy Courtney. It includes exhibition invitations and announcements; press releases; prospectuses; catalogs; curricula vitae; correspondence; newsletters; interviews; artist's statements; brochures; and news articles. Most of the material dates from the late 1980s through the 1990s, a period of growing interest and scholarship regarding artists' books in Great Britian.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Yale Center for British Art, Gift of Cathy Courtney

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Curator of Rare Books and Manuscripts.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open without restriction.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Cathy Courtney collection of artists' books ephemera, Yale Center for British Art, Gift of Cathy Courtney

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Cathy Courtney is a noted author and researcher on the topic of British artists' books. She became interested in artists' books in the 1980s after she encountered them in New York City. In 1983, she began to write about artists' books in a column for Art Monthly, covering the artists' books scene in Great Britian. Her column and subsequent publications surfaced the lively debates about what an artist book is or is not, highlighted the works of artists, and raised awareness about artists' books more broadly.

did.unittitle: University of Brighton Book Art Competition
